content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Updated `project create-from-bead` to keep milestone-plan membership bound to stable bead identity instead of mutable titles. In `src/cli/project.rs`, bead ownership checks now canonicalize milestone-scoped aliases, `resolve_bead_plan` matches plan entries with `milestone_bead_refs_match` so short live bead IDs still resolve against qualified stored IDs, and successful ID matches only emit a debug log when the stored title has drifted. Legacy title-only plans still fall back by title, but the warning is now persisted with a milestone-local marker file so operators see it once per milestone across CLI invocations, and the fallback continues to return the actionable regenerate-plan error when title drift makes matching impossible. The create-from-bead path still validates plan membership independently of `--flow`, while valid `--flow` overrides continue to control only flow selection. Regression coverage in `src/cli/project.rs` and `tests/cli.rs` now covers alias-tolerant stable ID lookup, persisted legacy-warning suppression across invocations, title-drift behavior, and the flow-override membership guard.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the existing `create-from-bead` lookup path, milestone bead-reference helpers, and regression tests to confirm the two pending failures: alias-sensitive ID matching and process-local legacy warning deduplication.
2. [Completed] Update bead-plan resolution and milestone ownership checks to canonicalize/compare milestone bead references by stable identity, so short live IDs and qualified stored IDs resolve as the same bead while preserving debug logging for title drift.
3. [Completed] Replace process-local legacy warning deduplication with a persisted per-milestone marker in the milestone workspace, and keep warning emission outside flow-selection logic so `--flow` cannot bypass membership validation.
4. [Completed] Add regression coverage for alias-tolerant ID resolution and cross-invocation legacy warning suppression, then run the required repository validation gates including `nix build`.

## Validation Evidence

- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub resolve_bead_plan_matches_short_live_bead_id_against_qualified_plan_bead_id -- --nocapture` succeeded.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub project_create_from_bead_warns_once_per_legacy_milestone_across_invocations -- --nocapture` succeeded.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub project_create_from_bead_rejects_invalid_plan_membership_even_with_flow_override -- --nocapture` succeeded.
- `nix develop -c cargo fmt --check` succeeded.
- `nix develop -c cargo clippy --locked -- -D warnings` succeeded.
- `nix develop -c cargo test --locked --features test-stub` succeeded: lib tests `1211 passed`, CLI tests `368 passed` with `3 ignored`, `run_attach_tmux` tests `1 passed`, unit tests `1079 passed` with `1 ignored`, doc tests clean with `9 ignored`.
- `nix build` succeeded.

## Outstanding Risks

- The worktree still contains unrelated live orchestration-state changes under `.ralph-burning/` that were left untouched and excluded from review per instructions.
